sections.lead: The Anthropic Cloud Architect exam requires 75% on 60 scenario questions, testing Claude's stateless nature and its four augmentation layers.

bullets.label: Exam Structure
bullets.text: The Anthropic Cloud Certified Architect exam has 60 multiple-choice scenario-based questions, requiring at least 75% correctness.

bullets.label: Exam Scenarios
bullets.text: The exam draws from six production scenarios, presenting test-takers with four randomly selected ones.

bullets.label: Claude Stateless
bullets.text: Claude is fundamentally a stateless language model accessed via the api.anthropic.com/v1/messages HTTP endpoint.

bullets.label: Core Limitations
bullets.text: Claude has no memory between calls, doesn't run loops, and does not execute code directly.

bullets.label: Four Layers
bullets.text: Claude's core engine is augmented by four layers: the Claude API, Agent SDK, Claude Code, and Model Context Protocol (MCP).

sections.lead: Implement agent loops for stateless Claude using stop_reason; for larger tasks, multi-agent systems and targeted retries are key.

bullets.label: Agent Loop
bullets.text: Your code must implement the agent loop for multi-step tasks because Claude is stateless.

bullets.label: Stop Reason
bullets.text: The stop_reason field, when calling messages.create with tools, guides the agent loop: end_turn means Claude is done, tool_use means a function needs execution.

bullets.label: Full Cycle
bullets.text: The full agent loop involves calling messages.create, inspecting tool_use, locally executing the function, appending tool_result, and recalling messages.create.

bullets.label: Multi-Agent
bullets.text: For tasks exceeding a single Claude's context budget, use multi-agent systems with a coordinator Claude spawning sub-agents via the task_tool.

bullets.label: Task Decomposition
bullets.text: Decompose tasks either sequentially for interdependent steps or adaptively for open-ended work.

bullets.label: Sub-Agent Retry
bullets.text: When a sub-agent fails, almost always use a targeted retry of only that failed agent to prevent unwanted side effects.

sections.lead: Claude Code uses hierarchical Claude.md files for session context, supports custom slash commands, and integrates with CI/CD via claude code -p.

bullets.label: Code Memory
bullets.text: Claude Code starts each session with no project memory, addressed by Claude.md files stitched into the system prompt.

bullets.label: User Level
bullets.text: The user level Claude.md file, located at ~/.claude/claude.md, is for personal settings and is not version controlled.

bullets.label: Project Level
bullets.text: The project level Claude.md file, found at repo root/claude.md, establishes team-wide conventions and is committed to Git.

bullets.label: Directory Level
bullets.text: The directory level Claude.md file, in sub-package/claude.md, defines rules specific to a particular folder.

bullets.label: Slash Commands
bullets.text: Custom slash commands, defined in .claude/commands/ with YAML front matter, offer reusable prompts and can constrain allowed_tools.

bullets.label: CI/CD Integration
bullets.text: For headless CI/CD, use claude code -p with a structured output schema, failing pipelines only on explicitly named violation categories.
